channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: BatteryPass-12K: The First Dataset for the Novel Digital Battery Passport Conformance Task
item.description: arXiv:2604.26986v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a novel task of digital battery passport (DBP) conformance classification and introduce the first public benchmark for the task: BatteryPass-12K, created synthetically from real pilot samples. This is as the EU's battery regulation on DBP...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, example, release, prompt, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CL-bench Life: Can Language Models Learn from Real-Life Context?
item.description: arXiv:2604.27043v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Today's AI assistants such as OpenClaw are designed to handle context effectively, making context learning an increasingly important capability for models. As these systems move beyond professional settings into everyday life, the nature of the contex...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Useless but Safe? Benchmarking Utility Recovery with User Intent Clarification in Multi-Turn Conversations
item.description: arXiv:2604.27093v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current LLM safety alignment techniques improve model robustness against adversarial attacks, but overlook whether and how LLMs can recover helpfulness when benign users clarify their intent. We introduce CarryOnBench, the first interactive benchmark ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, alignment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Exploring the Limits of Pruning: Task-Specific Neurons, Model Collapse, and Recovery in Task-Specific Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.27115v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neuron pruning is widely used to reduce the computational cost and parameter footprint of large language models, yet it remains unclear whether neurons in task-specific models contribute uniformly to task performance. In this work, we provide empirica...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, study, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cross-Lingual Response Consistency in Large Language Models: An ILR-Informed Evaluation of Claude Across Six Languages
item.description: arXiv:2604.27137v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper introduces a systematic evaluation framework grounded in the Interagency Language Roundtable (ILR) Skill Level Descriptions and applies it to Claude (Sonnet 4.6) across six languages: English, French, Romanian, Spanish, Italian, and German....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, large language model, analysis, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Semantic Structure of Feature Space in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.27169v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We show that the geometric relations between semantic features in large language models' hidden states closely mirror human psychological associations. We construct feature vectors corresponding to 360 words and project them on 32 semantic axes (e.g. ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, vector, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Path-Lock Expert: Separating Reasoning Mode in Hybrid Thinking via Architecture-Level Separation
item.description: arXiv:2604.27201v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hybrid-thinking language models expose explicit think and no-think modes, but current designs do not separate them cleanly. Even in no-think mode, models often emit long and self-reflective responses, causing reasoning leakage. Existing work reduces t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, attention, example, embedding, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Compositional Meta-Learning for Mitigating Task Heterogeneity in Physics-Informed Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.26999v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) approximate solutions of partial differential equations (PDEs) by embedding physical laws into the loss function. In parameterized PDE families, variations in coefficients or boundary/initial conditions define ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, embedding, model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Your LLM Reaches End-of-Life: A Framework for Confident Model Migration in Production Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.27082v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a framework for migrating production Large Language Model (LLM) based systems when the underlying model reaches end-of-life or requires replacement. The key contribution is a Bayesian statistical approach that calibrates automated evaluatio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, large language model, product, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: End-to-end autonomous scientific discovery on a real optical platform
item.description: arXiv:2604.27092v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific research has long been human-led, driving new knowledge and transformative technologies through the continual revision of questions, methods and claims as evidence accumulates. Although large language model (LLM)-based agents are beginning ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, attention, study, demonstration | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Think it, Run it: Autonomous ML pipeline generation via self-healing multi-agent AI
item.description: arXiv:2604.27096v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The purpose of our paper is to develop a unified multi-agent architecture that automates end-to-end machine learning (ML) pipeline generation from datasets and natural-language (NL) goals, improving efficiency, robustness and explainability. A five-ag...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, study, LLM, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Unsupervised Electrofacies Classification and Porosity Characterization in the Offshore Keta Basin Using Wireline Logs
item.description: arXiv:2604.27126v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study presents an unsupervised machine learning workflow for electrofacies analysis in the offshore Keta Basin, Ghana, where core data are scarce. Six standard wireline logs from Well~C were analysed over a depth interval comprising approximately...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, study, tool, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TRUST: A Framework for Decentralized AI Service v.0.1
item.description: arXiv:2604.27132v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) and Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) in high-stakes domains demand reliable verification, yet centralized approaches suffer four limitations: (1) Robustness, with single points of failure vulnerable to attacks and bias; (2) Scal...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, reasoning, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Monitoring Neural Training with Topology: A Footprint-Predictable Collapse Index
item.description: arXiv:2604.26984v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representational collapse, where embeddings become anisotropic and lose multi-scale structure, can erode downstream performance long before performance metrics react. We propose an online, topology-aware monitor for evolving neural representations tha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, release, ICL, embedding, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: People-Centred Medical Image Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.26991v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in data-centric medical AI have produced highly accurate diagnostic systems, but the emphasis on data curation and performance metrics has not translated into widespread clinical adoption. We conjecture that this limited uptake stems f...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, attention, image, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Continual Learning Moves to Memory: A Study of Experience Reuse in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.27003v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memory-augmented LLM agents offer an appealing shortcut to continual learning: rather than updating model parameters, they accumulate experience in external memory, seemingly sidestepping the stability-plasticity dilemma of parametric learning. We sho...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, study, memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Automatic Causal Fairness Analysis with LLM-Generated Reporting
item.description: arXiv:2604.27011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AutoML, intended as the process of automating the application of machine learning to real-world problems, is a key step for AI popularisation. Most AutoML frameworks are not accounting for the potential lack of fairness in the training data and in the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, example, zero-shot, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Fidelity, Diversity, and Privacy: A Multi-Dimensional LLM Evaluation for Clinical Data Augmentation
item.description: arXiv:2604.27014v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The scarcity of high-quality annotated medical data, particularly in mental health, poses a significant bottleneck for training robust machine learning models. Privacy regulations restrict data sharing, making synthetic data generation a promising alt...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, large language model, vision, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Detecting Clinical Discrepancies in Health Coaching Agents: A Dual-Stream Memory and Reconciliation Architecture
item.description: arXiv:2604.27045v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Model (LLM) agents transition from single-session tools to persistent systems managing longitudinal healthcare journeys, their memory architectures face a critical challenge: reconciling two imperfect sources of truth. The patient's ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, memory, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, context window, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, reasoning, LLM, vector, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Length Value Model: Scalable Value Pretraining for Token-Level Length Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2604.27039v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Token serves as the fundamental unit of computation in modern autoregressive models, and generation length directly influences both inference cost and reasoning performance. Despite its importance, existing approaches lack fine-grained length modeling...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, prompt, reasoning, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Binary Spiking Neural Networks as Causal Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.27007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We provide a causal analysis of Binary Spiking Neural Networks (BSNNs) to explain their behavior. We formally define a BSNN and represent its spiking activity as a binary causal model. Thanks to this causal representation, we are able to explain the o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Dynamic Adversarial Fine-Tuning Reorganizes Refusal Geometry
item.description: arXiv:2604.27019v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safety-aligned language models must refuse harmful requests without collapsing into broad over-refusal, but the training-time mechanisms behind this tradeoff remain unclear. Prior work characterizes refusal directions and jailbreak robustness, yet doe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, fine-tuning, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NORACL: Neurogenesis for Oracle-free Resource-Adaptive Continual Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.27031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In a continual learning setting, we require a model to be plastic enough to learn a new task and stable enough to not disturb previously learned capabilities. We argue that this dilemma has an architectural root. A finite network has limited represent...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vision, model, analysis, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cross-Subject Generalization for EEG Decoding: A Survey of Deep Learning Methods
item.description: arXiv:2604.27033v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning for cross-subject EEG decoding is hindered by high inter-subject variability, which introduces a severe domain shift between training and unseen test subjects. This survey presents a comprehensive review of deep learning methodologies sp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, alignment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Targeted Linguistic Analysis of Sign Language Models with Minimal Translation Pairs
item.description: arXiv:2604.27232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Models of sign language have historically lagged behind those for spoken language (text and speech). Recent work has greatly improved their performance on tasks like sign language translation and isolated sign recognition. However, it remains unclear ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Optimal Stop-Loss and Take-Profit Parameterization for Autonomous Trading Agent Swarm
item.description: arXiv:2604.27150v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous crypto trading systems often spend most of their design effort on finding entries, while exits are left to fixed rules that are rarely tested in a systematic way. This paper examines whether better stop-loss and take-profit settings can imp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, product, arxiv, study, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, prompt, prompt engineering, tool, step-by-step | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: Unpacking Vibe Coding: Help-Seeking Processes in Student-AI Interactions While Programming
item.description: arXiv:2604.27134v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative AI is reshaping higher education programming through vibe coding, where students collaborate with AI via natural language rather than writing code line-by-line. We conceptualize this practice as help-seeking, analyzing 19,418 interaction tu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, product, prompt, API, prompting | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Simple Self-Conditioning Adaptation for Masked Diffusion Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.26985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked diffusion models (MDMs) generate discrete sequences by iterative denoising under an absorbing masking process. In standard masked diffusion, if a token remains masked after a reverse update, the model discards its clean-state prediction for tha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, image, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
